prompt: When did Meridian open its Aberdeen office?
response:
[{"text": "# Meridian Robotics — Company Overview

Meridian Robotics is a fictional company invented for this test harness. It was
founded in 2019 in Tromsø, Norway, by Dr. Ada Solvang and Henrik Vale. The company
designs autonomous inspection drones for offshore wind farms.

Meridian's headquarters are in Tromsø, with a second engineering office in Aberdeen,
Scotland, opened in 2022. As of 2024 the company employs 140 people across the two
sites.", "source": "01_company_overview.md", "score": -0.5046800374984741}, {"text": "The company's mission statement is: "Keep humans off the turbine, keep the turbines
turning." Meridian is privately held and has never disclosed annual revenue publicly.", "source": "01_company_overview.md", "score": -0.6789167523384094}, {"text": "# Meridian Robotics — Product Line

Meridian sells two drone models and one software product.

## Kestrel-1
The Kestrel-1 is Meridian's entry-level inspection drone, released in 2020. It has a
flight time of 38 minutes on a single charge and a maximum wind tolerance of 14 metres
per second. The Kestrel-1 carries a 20-megapixel visible-light camera but no thermal
imaging.", "source": "02_products.md", "score": -0.7431310415267944}, {"text": "## Data handling
Inspection footage is uploaded to TurbineSight over an encrypted link within 24 hours of
each flight. Raw footage is retained for seven years to meet client audit requirements.
Personal data is never collected during inspections; the drones are pointed at turbines,
not people.

## Incident history
Meridian has recorded three minor incidents since 2019, all involving hard landings in
gusty conditions. No injuries have ever occurred. The full incident log is confidential
and is not shared outside the safety team.", "source": "03_operations_safety.md", "score": -0.7533618211746216}]
